excerpt: On the four blessed nights of the city of Allah, special and exalted apertures open—apertures distinct from the eighteen that unfold during the daily prayer hours, and revelations descend concerning the veils and boundaries of those companions who were born from the pit with him.
Mawlānā ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib has said that one should pray for the Ḥajj on the 17th, 19th, and 21st nights, because the names of those who go to Hajj are written on those nights.

Mawlānā Muḥammad Bāqir said: On the seventeenth night, two large groups of people meet—the preserved ones and the established ones—in which the trumpets of the other descend through the vertical light and enter the spiritual heart (the Imam) and the physical heart (the Kaaba).

On the nineteenth night, the names of the year-round delegations who are going to meet Allah are written, and in it the fate of sustenance and age is written. On this night, the number of forms is divided and it is decided which noble form will be given how many times, and the Imam arranges the forms in his temple according to the hierarchy.

On the twenty-first night, the prophets pass away through the veil of their executor. A Nāṭiq always dies through the veil of his waṣī, and for this reason the death of the waṣī always precedes the Nāṭiq, just as Aaron died before Moses.

Among the four blessed nights, the night of al-Qadr is the twenty-third night of the city of Allah, which is the most blessed and the best of them all. All the blessings that come individually on the 17th, 19th, and 21st nights are the collective radiance of all of them on this night, and Laylat al-Qadr has its own unique blessings which also shine on this night.

All four proofs of ʿAyn ʿAẓīma have been given the administration of one night. On these nights, the affairs of Sayyidnā Ḥamīd al-Dīn al-Kirmānī (seventeenth), Sayyidnā Muʾayyad (nineteenth), and Mawlānā Abū al-Barakāt (twenty-first) also ascend and descend. Some things are given and some things are gained.

Mawlātnā Fāṭima was born on the twentieth of Jumādā al-Awwal with a span of thirty-five years between her and the Messenger of Allah. Mawlānā ʿAlī was born ten years before the Prophet's receipt of prophethood, and Mawlātnā Fāṭima was born five years before the Prophet's receipt of the mission. She was born on Laylat al-Qadr when blessed with a special birth.

On the Day of Ghadir, when the Prophet handed over the trusts to ʿAlī, he passed away and vacated his place. ʿAlī succeeded him and adopted the veil of Mīm. There is an interval of ninety-two days from Ghadir to the twelfth of Rabīʿ al-Awwal, which equals the numerical value of Muḥammad. Although the Prophet departed, ʿAlī in truth died.

Mawlānā ʿAlī's proof is Mawlātnā Fāṭima al-Zahrā. The gathering of all the preserved ones assembled before the Holy Prophet. A gathering of all the Imāms assembled before Mawlānā ʿAlī. In like manner, a congregation of all nine executors gathered at Mawlātnā Fāṭima.

Mawlātnā Fāṭima bint Muḥammad is called Laylat al-Qadr, and Mawlātnā Fāṭima bint al-Mahdī is called Laylat al-Qadr al-Kubrā. On Laylat al-Qadr al-Kubrā, the fermentations and veils of the Fāṭimids and their congregation will ascend and descend.

Similarly, Mawlānā Muḥammad ibn Aḥmad ibn ʿAlī al-Ṭayyib (the twenty-third Imām) will illuminate the believers through the author of the ʿAsr. The Prophet said: Seek Laylat al-Qadr on the twenty-third night.
